title: PM Lane 316 - Project Miner Temp Power Post-Review Customer Delivery And Proof Admission Gate
authority_basis: Standing PM blocker authority and repo technical authority, used here to package the next truthful post-lane-315 blocker in repo-visible form so the branch does not drift from hosted-green customer-preview review into unadmitted delivery, finance, or source-writeback work.
activation_condition: The admitted Temp Power actuals plus customer-preview review first-write slice is already hosted-green.
Customer-preview review remains explicitly no-delivery: durable_delivery_event must remain false and delivery_proof_recorded must remain false unless a later separately admitted delivery lane exists.
There is no existing PM Lane 316 documenting the next admission boundary after hosted-green customer-preview review.
current_truth: Lane 315 closed the admitted Temp Power actuals plus customer-preview review first-write slice with both hosted seam URLs green.

The customer-preview review contract and readback chain already define the next adjacent business boundary: durable delivery is not yet admitted, later delivery proof expectations may be described only as placeholders, and delivery_proof_recorded must stay false until a separate delivery lane exists.

The accepted no-live delivery defaults already exist in Lane 291: PM approval before delivery, named customer PM or owner representative, controlled email or later approved portal, no durable delivery event in the current review slice, and later proof types such as email receipt, signed transmittal, or portal timestamp once separately admitted.

Finance/payroll/billing/invoice/accounting outputs and source writeback remain deferred placeholder groups and are not the nearest adjacent follow-on lane from the newly hosted-green customer-preview review slice.

The next truthful blocker is decision shape, not current implementation failure: the branch needs an explicit next-admission gate for customer delivery completion and durable proof recording so later work does not drift.
allowed_actions: Create repo-visible no-code packet, handoff, and operations note defining the next explicit admission boundary after lane 315.
Route the next adjacent candidate to customer delivery completion and durable proof recording only.
Keep finance, payroll, billing, invoice, accounting, external finance sync, and source writeback explicitly outside the next-admission gate.
not_allowed: No product code, route, payload, schema, or hosted surface changes.
No hosted or local POST that claims customer delivery occurred.
No durable delivery event recording, delivery proof recording, or customer commitment execution.
No finance, payroll, billing, invoice, accounting, external finance sync, or source writeback work.
No auth, ingress, DNS, or secret changes.
validation: Decision label, selected outcome, current truth, and blocked-domain boundary are present across touched lane 316 files
Canonical PM docs/status now point beyond lane 315 without implying delivery, finance, or source-writeback admission
git diff --check passes for the touched lane 316 and canonical PM doc surfaces
validation_result: PASS: lane 316 defines the next admission boundary after hosted-green customer-preview review and keeps delivery, finance, and source-writeback widening clearly separated.
